missing_requirements.evidence_quote: Considering an AccountApplication object, calling the complete() method when the object is in the pending state wouldn’t make sense if the finalizing state performs wrap-up behavior, such as creating the blog account if approved—it would first have to know whether the application was approved.
missing_requirements.suggested_requirement: The system shall prevent calling the complete() method when the object is in the pending state.

hallucinations.rewritten_requirement: The system shall execute wrap-up behavior in the finalizing state only after determining the approval status of the application.
hallucinations.reason: This requirement seems to be a combination of two separate ideas: preventing calling complete() and executing wrap-up behavior. It's not clear what the original intent was.

ambiguities.rewritten_requirement: The system shall execute wrap-up behavior in the finalizing state only after determining the approval status of the application.
ambiguities.reason: This requirement is ambiguous because it's not clear what 'wrap-up behavior' means. Is it creating a blog account, or something else?
